Welcome to the Forge Workbench
If this is your first time here: open Documentation and read the glossary, brief template, and workflow guide. That's about 20 minutes and will save you hours of confusion. Then come back here and click Scope & Sequence to start scoping a challenge.
Open Documentation Jump to Scope & Sequence
FORGE PREP · Curriculum Workbench

From a challenge brief to a scoped, sequenced year

From a challenge brief to a scoped, sequenced year. Build the scope and sequence with the v2.9.16 prompt, stage sessions onto the year calendar, and trace each session into the Hess matrix. Standards coverage and cognitive depth audit themselves as you go.

Source: Karin Hess, Cognitive Rigor Matrix (2009). Norman Webb, Depth-of-Knowledge Levels for Four Content Areas (2002). David Krathwohl, A Revision of Bloom’s Taxonomy (2002).
Curriculum builder
v2.9.16 prompt

Scope & Sequence

Brief → Prompt → JSON → Render

Where a Guide takes a challenge brief, assembles the current LLM prompt, pastes the JSON response back, and sees the full pin-anchored scope and sequence rendered with pre-stage blocks, pins, sub-units, sessions, the Cold Transfer Task, and a fitness check. Switch to fluency-lane mode on the page itself if you need a single-standard explicit-instruction lane instead.

Depth-of-learning lens
Per-session & year view

Bloom ×
Depth of Knowledge

After scope & sequence · before lesson planning

Plot every staged session on a Bloom row by a Depth-of-Knowledge column so you can see what kind of thinking each session asks for and how complex that thinking actually is. Catches the gap where a session's Bloom verb sounds rigorous but the underlying task is shallow, surfaces where the year skews toward recall vs. extended reasoning, and gives the Guide a target cell to backward-plan the lesson from.

About this tool

Built for the team designing learning at Forge Prep. The Cohort Calendar holds the year-long operating schedule across Fall, Winter, and Spring terms; the Hess Matrix reference holds the framework for placing each session on a cognitive-rigor coordinate.

Tags and any locally edited descriptions persist in your browser’s localStorage. Export to JSON from the calendar if you want a backup.

Built as an internal cognitive-rigor workbench for Forge Prep. The Hess Cognitive Rigor Matrix and the Webb Depth-of-Knowledge framework are drawn from the published work of Karin Hess, Norman Webb, Lorin Anderson, and David Krathwohl, used with citation.
← All references
Per-challenge tool · Forge Prep

Scope & Sequence

Three steps. Paste a challenge brief, assemble the v2.9.16 prompt, paste the LLM's JSON response back, and render the full pin-anchored scope and sequence.

1 Brief 2 Assemble 3 Paste JSON 4 Review & stage
Source: Forge Teaching Framework v1.0. Wiggins & McTighe, Understanding by Design (2005). Barak Rosenshine, Principles of Instruction (2012).
Step 1 · Assemble
Build the scope prompt
Paste a brief, add optional challenge texture, pick a generation mode, and assemble the LLM prompt. Once you have the JSON back from the LLM, paste it into Step 3 below.
Switch to fluency lane if you want standards-aligned explicit instruction across a chosen week range instead of a full pin-anchored challenge.
Texture library
Every per-challenge texture stored in forge.challenge.textures. Edit a name, load into the textbox above, duplicate, delete, or export individually.
Full mode is the one-shot. Chunked modes split into Part 1 → Part 2 → Part 3 for tighter session-count control. ?
Paste LLM JSON response
Paste the LLM's full JSON response (including the ```json code fence), or load it from a file. Use the chunked-parts merger if you generated in Part 1 → Part 2 → Part 3 mode.
(use this for large scopes — browser paste truncates at ~256KB)
(takes a Word export of any scope-and-sequence table or lesson list — finds L1 / L2 / L3 entries by density, no fixed header required)
Used when you generated in Part 1 + Part 2 + Part 3 mode. Click to combine three partial outputs into one scope.
Merge chunked parts
Paste each part's JSON below. The merger deep-merges Part 1 (pins + sub-units) with Part 2 (sessions on each sub-unit) and Part 3 (CTT + fitness + selfVerification), then writes the unified scope into the main paste textarea above.
Scope transforms
Deterministic bulk operations on the currently rendered scope. No LLM round-trip — these are pure mechanical edits. Apply writes back into the JSON paste box; click Review & stage to re-render.
Shift weeks
Move every pin, sub-unit, session, pre-stage, and CTT forward or back by N weeks. Negative values shift earlier.
weeks
Swap track
Flip every session's track on every session that matches. Useful when a scope was generated against the wrong cohort.
→
Dedupe inventory refs
Walks every session.inventoryRefs and removes duplicates. Harmless to apply.
Renumber sessions
Walks each sub-unit and renumbers session ids to <subUnit.id>.<n> starting at 1. Use after inserting or removing sessions to keep ids tidy.
Split undifferentiated sessions
Finds sessions that span all four grades (G5-G8) with no -jr/-sr suffix and no cohort override, then splits each into Pattern A: id-jr (G5/G6) + id-sr (G7/G8). Full-day Pin events and sessions that already carry an override block are left alone. The Guide differentiates the two halves after the split.
Find & replace across titles
Replaces a substring in every session.title across the scope (base + cohort overrides + CTT title). Title-only — never touches lessonObjective, description, or any other field. Use Preview to see how many titles change before applying.
Export
Lessons → JSON
Flatten this challenge's pin-anchored scope into a list of session objects. Filter by cohort, phase, or block; copy or download. Use for chat-roll context, lesson-planning downstream, or inter-tool handoff.
Cohort
Phase
Include
Format
Diagnostic · no LLM
Gap-check a skeleton
Paste a sub-unit + lesson skeleton with NJSLS codes; check it for out-of-band standards, deprecated CCSS codes, bias violations, and missing teach paths. No LLM round-trip, instant feedback.
One sub-unit per block · Lessons indented · Each line: L# — title — CODE1, CODE2
Per-challenge scope-and-sequence workflow built on the Forge Teaching Framework v1.0. The v2.9.11 prompt encodes Wiggins & McTighe's Understanding by Design alongside Rosenshine's Principles of Instruction, synced against the full Forge Guide starter pack (canonical schedule, seminar protocol, Yardsticks tensions, Challenge Design Diagnostic). The rendered output preserves the pin-anchored structure of the source spec. Saved year data lives in your browser's local storage under forge_ss_year.
← All references ← Challenge scope & sequence
Standards-aligned explicit instruction · Forge Prep

Fluency Lane

A focused series of Rosenshine-sequenced sessions on one standard, threaded into a specific week range alongside whichever challenge is running. Not a Forge challenge — no pins, no partner, no Cold Transfer Task. Problems get grounded in the anchor challenge's context so the lane reinforces both.

Source: Forge Teaching Framework v1.0. Rosenshine, Principles of Instruction (2012). Wood, Yardsticks (4th ed.).
Saved lanes
Fluency lane library
Switch back to the full Forge challenge flow if you need pins, a partner, audience, Cold Transfer Task, and fitness check instead.

The LLM returns a v2.0 scope shape with isFluencyLane: true. Paste it into the regular Scope & Sequence page's JSON box to render and stage — the renderer handles fluency lanes the same way it handles challenges.

Go to Scope & Sequence to paste & render →
Fluency lane workflow. Standards-aligned explicit instruction (Teach → Guided Practice → Independent Practice → Diagnostic), Rosenshine-sequenced, anchored thematically to whichever challenge is running in the same weeks. Output renders through the same Scope & Sequence pipeline so staged sessions appear on the Cohort Calendar alongside challenge sessions.

Year-long cohort schedule · Forge Prep

Cohort Calendar

Thirty-six weeks of cohort time, 5-minute quantum, Monday through Friday. Anchors locked at 9:00 movement, 9:30 standup, 12:00 lunch + activity, 3:30 electives, 3:50 closeout.

Source: year-long schedule per the Forge Teaching Framework v1.0. Session storage: your browser's local storage under forge_calendar_blocks. Pairs with: the Scope & Sequence page (stages sessions onto this grid).

Cognitive Rigor · Forge Prep

Cognitive Rigor Matrix

Bloom × Depth of Knowledge matrices. View by session (existing) or by standard (v2.9.15, generated autonomously by the scheduled NJSLS Hess task).

Source: Karin Hess, Cognitive Rigor Matrix (2009). Norman Webb, Depth-of-Knowledge Levels for Four Content Areas (2002). David Krathwohl, A Revision of Bloom's Taxonomy: An Overview (2002). See RIGOR_PROMPT.md in the repository for the rules governing cell content.
View by:
No per-standard Hess matrices have been generated yet. The scheduled NJSLS Hess task will populate this view autonomously.
Session-specific Bloom × Depth of Knowledge matrices generated via copy-paste workflow with Claude. Each cell is a full proposed lesson plan — anchored task, counter-example one level adjacent, Hess descriptors, rationale, citation. Results persist in browser local storage under forge.matrix.<sessionId>. Bloom verbs follow Anderson & Krathwohl (2001); depth of knowledge levels follow Webb (2002); descriptors are from the Hess Cognitive Rigor Matrix (2009).
← Back to hub
Cognitive Rigor · Forge Prep

Bloom × Depth of Knowledge

A live distribution of every tagged session in the Cohort Calendar, laid out on the Hess Cognitive Rigor Matrix. Click any cell to see its Hess descriptors and every session tagged at that coordinate.

Source: Karin Hess, Cognitive Rigor Matrix (2009). Norman Webb, Depth-of-Knowledge Levels for Four Content Areas (2002). David Krathwohl, A Revision of Bloom's Taxonomy: An Overview (2002).
The frame

Two frameworks, complementary rather than competing. Bloom's Taxonomy classifies what kind of cognitive activity is happening, ordered from simpler to more complex: Remember, Understand, Apply, Analyze, Evaluate, Create. Webb's Depth of Knowledge classifies how cognitively complex the activity is — Recall and Reproduction, Skills and Concepts, Strategic Thinking, Extended Thinking. Depth is not difficulty: memorizing one hundred vocabulary words is hard but stays at Level 1. Depth lives in reasoning, planning, transfer, evidence, and sustained thinking.

The Hess Cognitive Rigor Matrix crosses the two into a single six-by-four grid because the same Bloom verb can sit at very different depths. "Create" at Depth of Knowledge Level 1 is brainstorming a list. "Create" at Depth of Knowledge Level 4 is synthesizing a multi-source thesis with a real audience. The trap the matrix exists to catch: Bloom's hierarchy makes higher verbs sound prestigious, so a "Create a poster about volcanoes" assignment can read as advanced when students are mostly copying facts onto cardboard — high Bloom verb, low Depth of Knowledge, the appearance of rigor without the substance.

View
Built as an internal curriculum reference for Forge Prep. The Cognitive Rigor page synthesizes the work of Karin Hess, Norman Webb, Lorin Anderson, and David Krathwohl. Hess matrices reproduced with full author citation per khess@nciea.org. Bloom background drawn from Patricia Armstrong, Vanderbilt Center for Teaching, used under Creative Commons Attribution.
← Back to hub
Curriculum coverage · Forge Prep

Standards × Rigor

For every NJSLS standard the cohort touches, where on Bloom × Depth of Knowledge are the sessions tagged that claim it? Surfaces standards that get only DOK 1 attention when the curriculum could go further, and standards covered by zero tagged sessions.

Source: session standards drawn from the live block list; placements from forge.rigor.tags. NJSLS codes as recorded on each session.
Standards × Rigor (this challenge) NJSLS Library Coverage Audit Year Coverage Map

Outline heatmap

v2.9.15 · pre-prompt-gen

Paste any JSON below. The parser recursively extracts every NJSLS code it sees and renders a standards-coverage heatmap. No staging, no calendar, no scope generation. Use it for fast iteration on outline shape before committing to a full v2.9.15 prompt run.

Saved outlines
From .docx Drop in any Forge scope, brief, or coverage report. The parser extracts codes and their context.
Standards × rigor cross-view. Cell counts answer: "at what cognitive coordinate are sessions referencing this standard placed?" Zero-tagged standards are sessions that claim the code but haven't yet been placed on the matrix. The Standards Roles table below answers the orthogonal question: "for each standard, which challenge first-teaches it, which retrieve it, and which apply it?"

Standards reference · Forge Prep

NJSLS Standards Library

The full New Jersey Student Learning Standards — grades 5–8, and 3–8 for Social Studies. Browse by subject, filter by grade, or search any code or keyword.

Source: 2023 NJSLS for English Language Arts & Mathematics; 2020 frameworks for Science, Social Studies, Computer Science & Design Thinking, Career/Life Literacies, and Health & Physical Education. New Jersey Department of Education, Office of Standards. Standards quoted verbatim.
Standards × Rigor NJSLS Library Coverage Audit Year Coverage Map
Offline standards reference. Not an official publication — consult the New Jersey Department of Education for authoritative versions.

Coverage audit · Forge Prep

Standards Coverage Audit

A defensible picture of what the eight-week Lunch Challenge actually covers — and at what cognitive depth. Every NJSLS standard in English Language Arts, Mathematics, and Science, checked against the codes the cohort's sessions carry.

Source: session standards from the live block list (std and std_defensible fields); NJSLS denominator from the embedded 2023/2020 New Jersey Student Learning Standards database; cognitive-depth placements from forge.rigor.tags. All figures recompute from current data each time this page opens.
Standards × Rigor NJSLS Library Coverage Audit Year Coverage Map
View
Coverage = a standard code appears on at least one instructional session (math, ELA, science, challenge, or diagnostic). It is not a claim about mastery or time-on-task — only that the standard is named somewhere in the schedule. Uncovered standards are in-scope NJSLS standards no session references. Consult the New Jersey Department of Education for authoritative standards text.

Audit · Forge Prep

Activity Log

Every scope save, texture save, calendar stage, and patch application across the workbench. Useful when something drifted and you don't remember when — or for tracing back which generation produced the version you're staring at now.

Source: forge_activity_log in browser localStorage. Capped at 2000 most-recent entries (Phase 4.5 raise).
Filter View
Logged events: scope-saved (Save to year click), texture-saved (Save in texture controls), calendar-staged (Re-stage to calendar), patch-applied (Apply fix patch). Other events not yet hooked — see CHANGELOG follow-up notes.

Reference · Forge Prep

Documentation

Four reference documents — start here if you're new to the workbench, or use as quick reference. All four are markdown files in the outputs folder; this page is a directory.

Glossary
40 locked terms
Every locked Forge term that appears in the workbench and the v2.9.16 prompt. Pin, Sub-unit, CTT, Pattern A, cohort override, DOK floor, BIAS rule, Yardsticks, artifact-as-witness, declared standards manifest, and 32 more. Read this first.
forge_glossary.md
Brief template
Required + optional fields
A working example brief modeled on Big Dogs. Required fields (Title, Partner, Summary, Fit, Cosmic Education, Skills, Standards, Duration) and optional ones. Copy + edit to start a new challenge.
forge_brief_template.md
Workflow guide
8 steps end-to-end
Open Scope, paste brief, Assemble prompt, take to LLM, paste response, Review & stage, address blockers, adjacency-read & Stage. Troubleshooting at the end.
forge_workflow_guide.md
Flag reading guide
Blocker / fixme / polish
How to read the acceptance gate. Severity tiers, flag categories, common blocker and fixme flags with their causes and surgical fixes. When to acknowledge vs fix.
forge_flag_reading_guide.md
Also useful
CHANGELOG.md — Version-by-version evolution of the prompt and the tool. Read the most recent entries to see what's new.
v2.9.14 system prompt — Embedded in the workbench. Click Assemble prompt on the Scope page with an empty brief to see the full ~14,000-word prompt.
Texture catalog
Worked examples
Texture documents are the rich content layer between a brief and the LLM. They describe a challenge's operational reality: confirmed partner, named places, sub-unit-by-sub-unit cohort treatment, recurring artifacts, anti-contamination guards. Read one to understand the genre before writing your own. Save your in-progress textures to the per-user library on the Scope page.
Big Dogs (St. Hubert’s)
bigdogs_texture_v8.md
Senior-primary 8-week challenge. Bias pivot. Adoption campaign showcase. Includes K9-unit visit prep, Yardsticks notes for 11/13-year-olds, anti-contamination guards.
Garden Systems
garden_systems_texture_v1.md
Cohort-shared challenge. Sustainability + enterprise. Ratios first-teach surface (retrieved later in Big Dogs). Long-arc partner contact (campus garden).
Both files live in the outputs folder. Open in any markdown viewer. The Scope page's Texture library button manages your own saved textures and is separate from this reference catalog.
Starter year — export / import
Export your full workbench state (saved scopes, calendar blocks, linter flags, guides, rooms, NJSLS favorites) as a single JSON file. Hand it to a colleague who can import to bootstrap their workbench with the same starter year. Storage keys: forge_ss_year, forge_calendar_blocks, forge_linter_flags, forge_guides, forge_rooms, forge_njsls_favorites.
Import will overwrite existing storage keys with the imported content. The page reloads after import so the rehydrate-on-load picks up the new state. Save your current work first if you don't want to lose it.
Markdown source files live alongside this HTML in the outputs folder. Open them in any markdown viewer (VS Code, Obsidian, GitHub) for a better reading experience than this index page.

Audit · Forge Prep

Title Ledger

Year-wide table of every lesson title across every saved challenge. Useful for spotting reused or near-duplicate titles across the year before reviewers do, and for surfacing which sub-units in which challenges share a head noun. Read-only — edits happen in the originating scope.

Source: forge_ss_year in browser localStorage. Flattens every saved scope's pins[].subUnits[].sessions[] into one sortable list. Includes coldTransferTask. Cohort and phase tags derived from the source scope.
Filter
Roadmap follow-up: Phase 2 will add collision highlighting (rows where verb + object match another row), reserved-keyword head-noun tagging, and an inline title-edit hook. This Phase 4 ships the year-wide view only — read-only and additive, no existing rendering path touched.
← Back to hub
Diagnostic · Forge Prep

Scope Diff

Compare two scope JSONs to see what changed. Useful for: "what did v2.9.4 → v2.9.5 actually change on the same brief," "what did the LLM rewrite on the fix-prompt run," or "how does Juniors' generation differ from Seniors'."

Source: two arbitrary scope JSON pastes. Diff is keyed on pin / sub-unit / session id; field-level diffs surface for matched-id sessions. Auto-loads either side from saved scopes in forge_ss_year.
View
Field-level diff is intentionally narrow — surfaces changes in title, lessonObjective, duration, block, standards, cogLoadTag, observableCheck, challengeConnection, developmentalNote. Sub-unit and pin metadata diffs surface name + objective + tier + campaignLean changes.
← Back to hub
Year compressed view · Forge Prep

Year at-a-glance

Every saved challenge as a single row across all 35 weeks. Colored by phase — Preparation, Exploration, Presentation. The shape that fits in one screen. Useful for spotting overlap, pacing, and parallel load at the cohort level.

Source: saved scopes in forge_ss_year. Each pin contributes a colored cell at its yearWeek. Pairs with: the week-zoom Cohort Calendar and the Year Coverage Map.
Phase Preparation Exploration Presentation No pin

Cognitive load curve

Per-week count of heavy / medium / light sessions for each cohort. Walks every staged calendar block's cogLoadTag. Spikes in heavy load surface over-loaded weeks before they hit the cohort.

Compressed year view. Each cell is one pin in one week. Hover for pin name + phase; click to jump to that scope in Cohort Calendar (week-zoomed) or open it in Scope & Sequence.
← Back to Standards × Rigor
Year-level standards diagnostic · Forge Prep

Year Coverage Map

Every NJSLS standard touched across the year, where it's first-taught, where it's retrieved, and where the gaps are. Aggregates every saved scope in forge_ss_year against the NJSLS denominator. Updates live as new scopes are saved.

Source: all saved scopes in forge_ss_year; NJSLS denominator from njsls_standards.js. Pairs with: the single-challenge Coverage Audit.
Standards × Rigor (this challenge) NJSLS Library Coverage Audit Year Coverage Map
Filter
Year coverage diagnostic. The denominator is the NJSLS in-scope band for Forge Prep (grades 5-8 ELA/Math/Science plus 3-8 Social Studies and 5+8 CSDT/CLKS/HPE). Standards outside that band are not flagged uncovered.

Afternoon schedule · Forge Prep

Afternoon Electives

Manage afternoon elective offerings — title, instructor, capacity, eligible grade bands, day of week, time, term. Phase E1 covers offerings only; students, enrollments, conflict checking, and calendar wiring land in subsequent phases.

Storage: forge_electives_offerings in browser localStorage. Export to JSON from the toolbar to back up or share.
0 offerings
Phase E1: offerings data model only. Phase E2 adds student rosters and enrollments. Phase E3 adds conflict detection and CSV export. Phase E4 wires offerings onto the cohort calendar in the 3:30-4:30 PM window. Phase E5 covers term rollover, substitutions, and audit additions.
← Back to hub
Operations · Forge Prep

Guides, Rooms, Materials

Define the Guide roster and the room inventory, assign Guides and rooms to staged sessions, and roll up the week's materials list. Surfaces Guide load and room conflicts on the Cohort Calendar.

Storage: forge_guides, forge_rooms in browser localStorage. Materials read live from the staged calendar blocks.

Guide roster

A Guide is anyone who teaches a session. Initials are the short label used on calendar blocks; color is the side-stripe on assigned blocks. Sessions inherit their Guide from the LLM unless reassigned via the detail panel or right-click on a block.

Rooms & spaces

A room is any teaching space. Capacity is optional but enables an over-capacity warning. Sessions get a room assignment via the detail panel or right-click on a block. Room conflicts (two sessions, same room, overlapping time) surface on the Cohort Calendar.

Materials rollup

Walks every staged session in scope, pulls materials arrays from each block (and from Sub-unit / Pin metadata when available), and aggregates into a per-day list. Print the result for the Monday-morning supplies pile.

Partner roster

Real-world partners involved in challenges (St. Hubert's, Garden Center, Livingston PD K9 Unit, etc.). Status tracks outreach state. The "Partner facts" field is the text the workbench will auto-inject into a challenge brief when the brief mentions this partner — anchors the LLM in real, accurate detail without you having to retype it for every challenge.

Calendar conflicts

Walks every staged calendar block, finds pairs that overlap on time within the same week and share a Guide, room, or cohort. Surfaces them grouped by type. Fix by clicking through to the affected blocks on the Cohort Calendar and rescheduling there.

Guide load across the year

Total minutes per Guide per week across all staged sessions. Surfaces weeks where a Guide is over-loaded or under-loaded relative to the cohort.

Operations workbench. Guides + Rooms feed assignment dropdowns in the calendar's detail panel and via right-click. Materials view reads the live block list, so newly staged sessions appear immediately.

Keyboard shortcuts

Press ? from anywhere to open this. Press Esc to close.

Global

?Open this shortcut overlay
⌘+KQuick search across pages, scopes, partners, standards
EscClose overlays / modals

Cohort Calendar (#calendar)

← / →Previous / next week
⌘+ZUndo (calendar destructive action)
⌘+XCut selected blocks
⌘+CCopy selected blocks
⌘+VPaste blocks into the current week
DeleteDelete selected blocks
Right-clickOpen the block context menu (group / ungroup / lock / detail)
DragMove a block; drag the edge to resize

Scope & Sequence (#scope)

Click rowOpen session detail in the side panel
Generation modeFull vs Part 1 / Part 2 / Part 3 chunked assembly
Standards codeHover any NJSLS code for the full standard statement
